name: Anomalo Data Quality API
what_it_does: AI-powered data quality monitoring platform with REST API — automatically detecting anomalies in data warehouse tables using ML models, running configurable data quality checks, alerting on freshness and completeness issues, and providing root cause analysis for data quality incidents.
use_cases: Querying data quality check results and anomaly history for data incident reporting
Managing automated data quality check configurations for warehouse tables via API
Integrating Anomalo alerts with Slack, PagerDuty, or JIRA for data team on-call
Pulling data quality scores for tables to surface in data catalog or governance tools
Automating table onboarding to Anomalo monitoring when new dbt models are created
not_for: Manual SQL-based data quality rules (use Soda or Great Expectations)
ETL pipeline orchestration and data transformation
best_when: A data team running Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, or Redshift wants AI-powered automatic anomaly detection on tables without writing individual rules for every check.
avoid_when: You need explicit, auditable rule-based checks for compliance (use Soda), prefer open-source (use Great Expectations), or want catalog features alongside quality (use Atlan or Alation).

auth.notes: API token from Anomalo account settings. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped access. Webhook for check failure and anomaly detection events. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Warehouse credentials stored by Anomalo for monitoring access.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing; table-based or data-volume pricing
pricing.notes: San Francisco-based company. Founded 2019. Raised $25M+. Sequoia and Index Ventures backed. AI anomaly detection on time-series metrics per table. Freshness, volume, distribution, and schema checks automatically configured. dbt integration for model-level data quality. Snowflake Partner Connect integration.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for tables, checks, alerts, and anomalies. Bearer token auth. Good API documentation. Table monitoring requires warehouse connection setup. ML anomaly thresholds are auto-configured — query API to understand current thresholds. Check run history queryable by table and time range.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Check reads fully idempotent. Alert reads idempotent. Table configuration reads idempotent. Anomaly event reads idempotent by event ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Warehouse connection setup required before table monitoring begins
ML thresholds are auto-configured — initial learning period before useful anomalies
Table IDs use warehouse-specific identifiers (database.schema.table format)
Check history has retention limits — export important history before expiry
dbt integration for model-level monitoring requires dbt metadata feed setup

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. US data residency. Bearer token auth. Warehouse credentials stored by Anomalo — evaluate trust model for sensitive data. Read-only warehouse access for monitoring. Anomaly data includes metadata only, not raw data rows.
